skill.name: cqrs-implementation
skill.description: Implement Command Query Responsibility Segregation for scalable architectures. Use when separating read and write models, optimizing query performance, or building event-sourced systems.
skill.summary: Implement Command Query Responsibility Segregation for scalable architectures. Use when separating r...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 30 potential issues, but all are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code fences as shell execution, database queries as network fetches, documentation URLs as suspicious, and sort order strings as weak cryptography. This is a legitimate CQRS educational skill with Python code templates. No actual security risks identified.

content.value_statement: Building applications that need to scale reads and writes independently is challenging with traditional CRUD patterns. This skill provides ready-to-use CQRS templates and patterns to separate command and query responsibilities, enabling optimized performance for both write-intensive and read-heavy workloads.
actual_capabilities: Guide implementation of Command and Query separation in Python applications
Provide reusable command bus and handler templates with validation patterns
Supply query bus templates with pagination and filtering support
Show read model synchronization patterns with event projectors
Demonstrate eventual consistency handling for read-your-writes scenarios
Include FastAPI integration examples for RESTful CQRS endpoints
limitations: Templates are educational starting points, not production-ready implementations
Does not include event store implementation details
Assumes basic familiarity with async Python and FastAPI
Does not cover domain-driven design extensively

use_cases.title: Build High-Performance E-commerce Platform
use_cases.description: Separate order processing (writes) from product catalog browsing (reads) to handle Black Friday traffic spikes with different scaling strategies.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers building scalable e-commerce systems

use_cases.title: Implement Real-time Analytics Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create optimized read models for complex aggregation queries while keeping write operations simple for data ingestion pipelines.

use_cases.title: Migrate Monolithic App to Event-Sourced Architecture
use_cases.description: Introduce CQRS incrementally to separate concerns and prepare for future event sourcing capabilities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic CQRS Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a basic CQRS structure in Python. I need command and query base classes, a command bus, and simple handlers for a user management domain.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project that needs command/query separation

prompt_templates.title: Read Model Projection
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to implement a read model projection that keeps a denormalized user profile in sync with domain events. Include checkpoint management for resilience.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building read models from event streams

prompt_templates.title: CQRS with FastAPI
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI application with separate POST/DELETE endpoints for commands and GET endpoints for queries, using dependency injection for command and query buses.

prompt_templates.title: Eventual Consistency Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a query handler that can wait for read model synchronization after a write operation, with timeout and stale data warning for read-your-writes consistency.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling consistency between commands and queries

output_examples.input: How do I separate read and write operations in my Python application?
output: CQRS separates your application into two distinct models: Commands (write operations that change state) and Queries (read operations that return data). The skill provides Python templates showing how to implement Command and Query handlers, a command bus for dispatching, and separate read models optimized for specific query patterns.

Key components include: Command base class with validation, CommandHandler interface, CommandBus for routing, Query base class, QueryHandler for data retrieval, and ReadModelSynchronizer for keeping views updated.

output_examples.input: Show me how to handle the delay between writing data and reading it back
output: This is called eventual consistency. The skill includes a ConsistentQueryHandler pattern that can wait for the read model to catch up after a write. It polls the projection version and retries until it reaches the expected version, with a configurable timeout.
If the timeout expires, it returns the stale data with a warning flag so your application can decide whether to proceed or notify the user.

best_practices: Start with a clear boundary between commands and queries - do not mix read and write logic in the same handler
Design read models specifically for their query patterns rather than trying to create a one-size-fits-all view
Implement idempotent command handlers to enable safe retries during network failures
anti_patterns: Querying the write database inside command handlers - commands should only validate and persist, never read for business logic
Creating a single read model that tries to serve all query patterns - this defeats the purpose of optimized read models
Ignoring eventual consistency and assuming immediate read-after-write availability without proper handling

faq.question: What is CQRS?
faq.answer: CQRS stands for Command Query Responsibility Segregation. It is an architectural pattern that separates read operations (queries) from write operations (commands), allowing each to be optimized independently.

faq.question: When should I use CQRS?
faq.answer: Use CQRS when you have different scaling requirements for reads and writes, complex query patterns that benefit from denormalized views, or when building event-sourced systems.

faq.question: Does CQRS require event sourcing?
faq.answer: No, CQRS can be used independently of event sourcing. Event sourcing is a complementary pattern that stores state changes as events, while CQRS focuses on separating command and query models.

faq.question: What is eventual consistency in CQRS?
faq.answer: Eventual consistency means that after a command modifies data, there may be a delay before the read model reflects that change. This is normal in CQRS and requires designing your application to handle stale data.

faq.question: Can CQRS be used with REST APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes FastAPI templates showing how to use POST, PUT, DELETE for commands and GET for queries, with proper separation of command and query buses.

faq.question: What are the main components of CQRS?
faq.answer: The main components are: Command (intent to change state), Command Handler (validates and executes), Event (record of change), Query (data request), Query Handler (retrieves data), and Projector (updates read model).
